component.name: Canva Linux
component.summary: A community opensource desktop wrapper for use with Canva

p.em: Canva Linux is a community-maintained open source package for use with Canva and is not published, verified, endorsed, certified, or officially supported by Canva Pty Ltd.

description.p: Canva Linux provides a Linux desktop wrapper around Canva with persistent sessions, internal tabs, OAuth popup handling, Flatpak packaging, upload/export workflows, media support, and desktop integration.

screenshot.caption: Custom eyedropper workflow inside Canva Linux

description.p: Converted the main Electron entrypoint to TypeScript while preserving CommonJS runtime behavior, startup wiring, tabs, OAuth, GPU diagnostics, session handling, and the current EyeDropper bridge.
Realigned Canva Linux versioning to the official alpha series, updated documentation and metadata, and adjusted the TypeScript migration roadmap.

Performed a final Flathub and release-readiness validation pass, reviewed AppStream metadata and packaging documentation, and intentionally left runtime behavior unchanged before preparing 1.4.9-rc.1.

Prepared AppStream screenshot integration around the real local screenshot set staged in the repository, updated packaging documentation, and intentionally kept screenshot URLs out of MetaInfo until stable tag, commit, or release URLs exist.
Refocused the 1.4.10-dev.X cycle on Flathub validation and packaging-readiness, reviewed metadata and manifest documentation, and intentionally kept runtime behavior unchanged.
Made native Wayland the preferred session mode, kept X11 as a manual fallback, merged the fixed Home-tab shell UI, and cleaned the package icon sources.

Fixed the custom eyedropper regression after preload modularization by generating a single runtime preload bundle from the modular source files, preserving the bundled custom colorpicker path in the Canva editor.
Made the Home tab fixed and non-closable, removed the new-tab button, updated the shell documentation in English, and switched the project license to GNU GPL v3.0-or-later.
